title:
Evaluation of Push-Out Bond Strength and Penetrability of Heat Resistant Bioceramic Sealer Obturated in Lateral vs Vertical Compaction Techniques in Mandibular Premolars

(A Comparative In-Vitro Study)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"165vs","text":"Background: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xorr3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sdevs","text":"The main goal of root canal treatment is to provide three-dimensional obturation of the root canal system.Root canal sealers are necessary to seal the gap between the root dentin wall and the obturating material.Calcium silicate-based materials have attracted considerable attention because of their good biocompatibility and bioactivity,enhancing the formation of crystalline structure similar to tooth and bone apatite, thereby improving sealer-to-root dentin bonding. The waterbased hydraulic calcium silicate sealers cannot be used with the warm root canal obturation techniques because heat dramatically changes the properties of the materials including setting time, flowability, viscosity and weight loss.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atagk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lm6lc","text":"Aim:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kmccb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ugv27","text":"The aim of this study is to compare between the Ceraseal and Neosealer flo in terms of pushout bond strength and penetrability when each is used in either lateral or vertical compaction technique in single-rooted mandibular premolars.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"athuv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q75qq","text":"Methods:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cahq4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cwolp","text":"Sixty human permanent extracted single-rooted mandibular premolar teeth will be decoronated and instrumented till size (40/04) by M3 Pro gold files with standard irrigation protocol. The specimens will then be randomly divided into 4 groups(n=15) according to the type of root canal sealer used and the obturation technique employed:Group 1 (CL): Ceraseal in lateral compaction. Group 2 (CV): Ceraseal in warm vertical compaction. Group 3 (NL): Neosealer flo in lateral compaction. Group 4 (NV): Neosealer flo in warm vertical compaction.Teeth will be sectioned  horizontally into 2mm thick slices using diamond disc at marks done at 3mm and 7 mm at apical and middle parts respectively.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ucg3f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"r7cqo","text":"Then the specimens will be tested for push out bond strength Each specimen will be placed on the base of the Universal Testing Machine. Also after 72 hours from obturation, each tooth will be embedded centrally and vertically in an acrylic resin block the sections will be imaged using a confocal laser scanning microscope at 10X magnification for evaluation of penetrability.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c1gwi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The external root surfaces of the teeth will be cleaned with a curette to remove calculus and hard deposits and then will be soaked in 5.25% Sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) for 30 minutes to remove soft tissue debris. The teeth will then be stored in sterile saline till use.
Pre-operative radiographs will be taken from buccolingual and mesiodistal aspects to assess the presence of a single, mature canal and absence of calcifications, anomalies or resorption.
The teeth will be decoronated using a low-speed diamond saw under copious irrigation to obtain a standardized root length of 16 mm.

K-file (size #10 / 0.02 taper) will be inserted into the canal to check patency. Then the working length will be adjusted using K-file (size#15 / 0.02 taper) until showing from the apex and then subtracting 1mm to obtain a standard working length of 15 mm.

Each root canal will be prepared using a crown-down technique with the 6 files Dental M3 Pro Gold System. M3 Pro Gold opener (size #18 / 0.04 taper) will be mounted on a C-Smart 1 pilot and will be inserted into the canal at speed of 300 rpm and torque 1.5 N cm. The second file M3 Pro Gold(size #20 / 0.04 taper) will be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm. The third file M3 Pro Gold (size #25 / 0.06 taper) will be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm. This will be followed by the fourth file M3 Pro Gold (size #30 / 0.04 taper) to be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm. The fifth file M3 Pro Gold (size #35 / 0.04 taper) will be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm while file (size #40 / 0.04 taper) will be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm as a final preparatory file.

After each instrument, the canal will be irrigated using 2 ml 2.5% sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) solution in a 27-gauge slotted, side-vented needle. After complete preparation, the root canal will be rinsed with 2 ml of 17% ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) solution for 1 min to remove the smear layer. As a final irrigation, 10 ml of distilled water will be used.

Each canal will be dried with absorbent paper points (size #40 / 0.04 taper). A radiograph will be obtained to ensure proper master cone extension (size #40 / 0.04 taper). Each canal will be dried again using paper points.

The specimens will then be randomly divided into 4 groups(n=15) according to the type of root canal sealer used and the obturation technique employed: Group 1 (CL): Ceraseal in lateral compaction. Group 2 (CV): Ceraseal in vertical compaction. Group 3 (NL): Neosealer flo in lateral compaction. Group 4 (NV): Neosealer flo in vertical compaction.

Each tooth will be marked at 3mm and 7mm from the root apex to identify the apical and middle slices respectively. The tooth will be then horizontally sectioned into 2mm thick slices using diamond disc at these marks. To maintain the uniformity of the sample, the thickness of each slice will be measured utilizing Vernier caliper. Each specimen will be placed on the base of the Universal Testing Machine (Instron, Norwood, MA, USA) with the apical end facing the load cell for the punch affixed to the crosshead with a speed of 0.5 mm per minute until extrusion of the obturation. The push-out bond strength value will then be calculated using computer software connected to the universal testing machine.

After 72 hours from obturation, each tooth will be embedded centrally and vertically in an acrylic resin block. Each root will be horizontally sectioned using a diamond disc removing 1-mm-thick sections at 3 and 7 mm from the root apex representing the apical and middle thirds respectively. The coronal-facing surface of each section will be polished for 10 seconds on each side. The polished sections will be imaged using a confocal laser scanning microscope at 10X magnification. Images of each sample will be analyzed and evaluated.
